verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine with no transmembrane domain. Its canonical fate is secretion as a large latent complex (LLC with LTBP) into the ECM. However, on activated FOXP3+ Tregs, platelets, and TLR-stimulated B cells, latent TGF-β1 is stably tethered to the outer leaflet via a disulfide-linked, wash-resistant complex with GARP (LRRC32), a type-I TM protein. GARP knockdown abolishes surface LAP/TGF-β1. This constitutes genuine, stable, cell-surface-accessible display — but only in these restricted cell activation states. The surface form is the GARP-tethered LAP/TGF-β1 complex. Verdict: contextual / stable_surface_attachment (cell-state/activation-induced in immune cells).
